bio.short: I've been in IT since the early 1990s — starting with network engineering, IP/IPX protocols, and corporate infrastructure. Over the following decades I built e-commerce operations, wrote three engineering books on network architecture, and launched a portfolio of bootstrapped internet products.
long: My work today centers on data collection infrastructure, click fraud defense, and building durable internet businesses without venture capital. The products I've built — clickfraud.ru, xmldatafeed.com, offlinecrm.ru, tapki.com — each solve a specific, unglamorous problem at the intersection of internet operations and data.

I write and speak in public because I find most conversation about IT entrepreneurship in Russia to be full of marketing noise and success theater. The channel bezsmuzi ('without smoothie') exists to provide a counterpoint: real revenue figures, real mistakes, real operating decisions.

values.title: Evidence over theory
values.desc: Revenue numbers, traffic data, and failure post-mortems are more useful than frameworks and methodology names.

values.title: Long game
values.desc: Building in Russia since the 1990s means you've seen markets, regulations, and technologies change completely. Durability matters more than growth hacks.

values.title: Technical clarity
values.desc: Engineering thinking applied to business problems: understand the system, find the bottleneck, fix it. Repeat.

values.title: No pretense
values.desc: The channel isn't called 'without smoothie' for decoration. Honest reporting on what works and what doesn't is rare, and valuable.

books.title: IP/IPX Traffic Switching and Routing
books.desc: Professional engineering reference on routing and switching. Written for network administrators, not marketers.

books.title: Corporate Network Technologies
books.desc: Comprehensive reference on building and operating corporate networks in Russian infrastructure contexts.

books.title: Building Computer Networks. For Professionals
books.desc: Practical guide to designing, deploying, and maintaining computer networks. Reviewed by Kompyuterra as an essential resource for Russian network engineers.

timeline.label: State grant
timeline.detail: 8M RUB from Foundation for Innovation Assistance
